raw:
- I want to build another page in the degisn tab that focuses on plans. I want this page to display the plan.md files for each project in an easily parsable and adjustable way. I want the user to be able to designate certain features as delivery milestones, manually defer or delay features, manually prioritize features. In general I want the user to be as in the loop about the dev plan as possible. I also want them to be able to interactively design the plan and have the PM adjust to their changes.

- the skills in the workforce tab ate alll named wrong. The names dont corrospond to the content of the skill. It seems the first lne of the skill files dont contain a name so orrchestrator is just diusplaying the first line, which is wrong.

- The MCP pace in the workforce page only lists github. It isnt updating with the actual MCP servers that are available and connected.

- In general it seems that the library and workforce displays were not fully plugged into their folders on the file system, so they arent a display of the live operability of each feature. The whole point of displaying them here is for orrcehstrator to be a reliable source of truth about what the ai development workflow is doing and what it has access to. I need orrchestrator to be more dynamic.

- There is no way for me to select the panel on the right half of the screen to scroll it or otherwise. Most panels contain more content than they display but they are rarely selectable or scrollable. This needs to be automated behaviour. It should never have to be a "fix" to make a page scroll. If the content is more than the display can display then the scrolling should be initiated automatically by the architecture of the program. Make certain that our foundational code is taking care of things like this.

- In addition to the previous, a lot of the time shoprtcuts are displayed that dont actually do anything. For example I am looking at design/workforce/MCP and I am given to option to press shift+tab to switch to the next panel but nothing happens when I do.

- I want the workflows in the workforce tab to refactor into human readable versions when the user wants to edit them. And then whent he user is done refactor them back to the compressed machine instructions.
optimized:
### OPT-001: Add Design > Plans panel with interactive plan management
Add a new sub-tab under Design top bar labeled "Plans" (alongside Intentions, Workforce, Library). Panel displays `PLAN.md` for each project in a structured, parseable view. Support per-feature actions: mark as delivery milestone, defer/delay, set priority. Allow interactive plan editing; changes propagate to the PM agent for incorporation. Maximize user visibility into the dev plan state.

Requirements:
- Enumerate projects with `PLAN.md` files (reuse project discovery from Oversee panel)
- Parse `[ ]` / `[x]` items and section headers; render as expandable tree or table
- Provide keybindings: toggle milestone, defer, prioritize up/down, edit in place
- Persist user changes back to `PLAN.md` (preserve markdown structure)
- Trigger PM agent on changes to reconcile plan
- Use existing scroll architecture (see OPT-005)

Source: Instruction 1 (Plans page in Design tab)

### OPT-002: Fix skill name display in Workforce > Skills tab
Skills in Design > Workforce > Skills tab currently display the first line of each skill `.md` file as the name, which is wrong because skill files do not start with a name line. Fix the skill loader in `orrch-library` (or wherever skills are parsed) to derive the display name from either (a) YAML frontmatter `name:` field, (b) the filename stem, or (c) the first `# Heading` in the body — in that precedence order. Update all existing skill files in `library/skills/` to include a proper name source if missing. Verify every skill in the Skills tab shows a name that corresponds to its actual content.

Source: Instruction 2 (skills named wrong)

### OPT-003: Wire Workforce > MCP tab to live MCP server list
Design > Workforce > MCP tab currently shows only "github" hardcoded. Replace with a live enumeration of all MCP servers currently available and connected to orrchestrator. Data source: scan `~/.claude.json`, `~/.config/claude/mcp.json`, project-local `.mcp.json`, and any orrchestrator-managed MCP config (`library/mcp/`). Display: server name, connection status (connected/disconnected), transport type, and tool count. Refresh on panel focus.

Source: Instruction 3 (MCP page only lists github)

### OPT-004: Make Library and Workforce panels dynamic file-system-backed views
Audit every sub-tab in Design > Library and Design > Workforce (Workflows, Teams, Agents, Skills, Tools, MCP, Profiles, Training, Models, plus Library sub-tabs). Confirm each panel reads its corresponding directory on disk at runtime and reflects the actual files present, not a hardcoded or stale in-memory list. Any panel that is not live-backed must be rewired to scan its source directory on open/refresh. Orrchestrator must be a reliable source of truth for what the AI dev workflow actually has access to. Add a refresh keybind (`r`) to each panel to force re-scan.

Acceptance: adding/removing a file in `library/skills/`, `workforces/`, `operations/`, `library/models/`, etc., is reflected in the UI without rebuild.

Source: Instruction 4 (library/workforce displays not plugged into filesystem)

### OPT-005: Implement automatic scroll architecture for all panels
Foundational fix: the TUI panel framework must automatically enable scrolling whenever rendered content exceeds the visible viewport height. This should NOT be an opt-in per-panel fix — make it a trait/wrapper in `orrch-tui` that every panel uses. Requirements:
- Detect content overflow at render time
- Auto-attach a vertical scroll state to any overflowing panel
- Allow focus to move to right-half panels (fix panel focus navigation so the right pane is selectable)
- Standard keybinds: `Up`/`Down`, `PgUp`/`PgDn`, `Home`/`End`, mouse wheel
- Scroll indicator (e.g., `▲▼` or position bar) when content overflows
- Refactor existing panels to use the new wrapper; remove any ad-hoc scroll logic

Acceptance: no panel in the TUI displays clipped content without a working scroll path. Right-half panels are focusable and scrollable.

Source: Instruction 5 (no way to select/scroll right-half panels)

### OPT-006: Audit and remove dead keybind hints
Audit all panels for displayed shortcut hints (e.g., `Shift+Tab` in Design > Workforce > MCP) that do not actually trigger the advertised action. For each dead hint: either wire up the keybind to do what the hint claims, or remove the hint from the footer/help line. Specific reported case: `Shift+Tab` in Design > Workforce > MCP does nothing — fix by either implementing panel switching or removing the hint. Add a sanity test that every footer-advertised keybind resolves to a registered handler.

Source: Instruction 6 (shortcuts shown that don't do anything, Shift+Tab example)

### OPT-007: Human-readable workflow edit mode with roundtrip compression
In Design > Workforce > Workflows, when the user enters edit mode on a workflow, expand the compressed pipe-delimited step table into a human-readable markdown form (e.g., numbered step list with labeled fields: agent, input, output, parallel group). On save/exit, re-compress back into the canonical machine-readable pipe-delimited format used by `orrch-workforce` parser. The on-disk format stays compressed; only the edit buffer expands.

Requirements:
- Lossless roundtrip (compressed → expanded → compressed produces byte-identical output for unchanged content)
- Expansion handled by `orrch-workforce` parser/formatter
- Edit mode launches vim or in-TUI editor with the expanded form
- Validate re-compression before writing to disk; show errors if malformed

Source: Instruction 7 (workflows human-readable edit mode)
